current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.
reasoning: Water is very warm for trout.
Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.
Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

recommended_flies.name: Egg Pattern
recommended_flies.reason: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough due to very warm water conditions. Recent stocking improves prospects with eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs recommended.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is the morning of day 2 because cooler morning temperatures and moderate sky cover create better conditions despite the warm water, supporting a balanced nymph approach.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS water temperature is 80.6°F with a flow of 97.7 cfs, gage height at 3.69 ft, and turbidity at 7.1.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages around 20%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with no strong trend, winds blow from the west at approximately 4.8 mph with gusts up to 16.1 mph, and there is a low chance of rain.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek cold-water refuges and oxygenated areas while holding near runs, seams, and structure. Present clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors such as egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracells. Exercise caution as warm water stresses trout and may reduce activity.
